steps.text: The equation \(21h + 10c = 900\) models total earnings in dollars. Each term is (rate) × (hours), so each coefficient represents a dollar-per-hour rate.
The variable \(h\) is defined as hours worked at the regular job. The number 21 is multiplied by \(h\), so 21 is the hourly rate (dollars per hour) at the regular job.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: "The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job" — 21 is the coefficient of \(h\) (regular job hours), so this is the hourly rate for the regular job. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: Assigns 21 to the second job, but 21 multiplies \(h\) (regular job), not \(c\) (second job). Eliminated.
  • C: Interprets 21 as a count of hours, not a rate. Eliminated.
  • D: Interprets 21 as hours at the second job; 21 doesn't multiply \(c\). Eliminated.
